  • API Reference
  • dyana.decode
  • dyana.decode.decoder

dyana.decode.decoder#

Structured decoder with constrained Viterbi over the Axis 2 state set.

Functions

decode_diagnostics(states)

Compute deterministic counters from decoded base-state sequence.

decode_with_constraints(log_scores, *[, ...])

Decode a path respecting transition penalties and minimum durations.

expand_scores(base_scores, expanded_states)

Copy base-state scores onto expanded substates.

random_scores(T[, seed])

Deterministic random log-scores for testing.

scripted_block_scores(blocks[, margin])

Build deterministic scores where each block favors a given state.

viterbi_decode(scores, transition[, initial])

Run a standard Viterbi dynamic program in log space.
dyana.decode.decoder.decode_diagnostics(states)[source]#

Compute deterministic counters from decoded base-state sequence.

Returns:

Contains ipu_start_after_leak_count.

Return type:

dict

Parameters:

states (Sequence[str])

dyana.decode.decoder.decode_with_constraints(log_scores, *, min_durations=None, transition=None, initial=None, tuning_params=None)[source]#

Decode a path respecting transition penalties and minimum durations.

Parameters:

  • log_scores (ndarray) – Emission log-scores on base states, shape (T, S_base).

  • min_durations (Dict[str, int] | None) – Optional overrides for minimum durations per base state.

  • transition (ndarray | None) – Optional base transition matrix; if None the default is used.

  • initial (ndarray | None) – Optional initial log-scores.

  • tuning_params (DecodeTuningParams | None)

Returns:

Decoded base-state names of length T.

Return type:

path_names

dyana.decode.decoder.expand_scores(base_scores, expanded_states)[source]#

Copy base-state scores onto expanded substates.

Parameters:

  • base_scores (ndarray)

  • expanded_states (Sequence[Tuple[str, int]])

Return type:

ndarray

dyana.decode.decoder.random_scores(T, seed=0)[source]#

Deterministic random log-scores for testing.

Parameters:

  • T (int)

  • seed (int)

Return type:

ndarray

dyana.decode.decoder.scripted_block_scores(blocks, margin=5.0)[source]#

Build deterministic scores where each block favors a given state.

Parameters:

  • blocks (Sequence[Tuple[str, int]]) – Sequence of (state_name, length) blocks.

  • margin (float) – Positive margin added to the favored state.

Return type:

ndarray

dyana.decode.decoder.viterbi_decode(scores, transition, initial=None)[source]#

Run a standard Viterbi dynamic program in log space.

Parameters:

  • scores (ndarray) – Emission log-scores, shape (T, S).

  • transition (ndarray) – Transition log-penalties, shape (S, S) with src on rows, dst on cols.

  • initial (ndarray | None) – Initial log-score per state, shape (S,). If None, zeros are used.

Returns:

  • path – Argmax state indices of length T.

  • path_score – Log-score of the best path.

Return type:

Tuple[List[int], float]

  • decode_with_constraints()
